name: top-k filtered multinomial draw (mask top-k, renormalize, inverse-CDF sample)

op.signature: token_ids[B] int32 = inverse_cdf_sample(renorm(top_k_mask(probs[B,V] fp32, top_k)), uniform_samples[B] fp32)

preconditions: 1 <= top_k <= V (top_k is a dispatch kwarg, not a tensor shape, so it cannot bind in the decidable constraint mini-language; the reference asserts it at runtime)
uniform_samples are in [0, 1) and are an INPUT (external RNG) -- see sampling_from_probs@1.0.0 for the determinism contract

the top-k tokens are selected by DESCENDING probability, ties broken by ASCENDING vocab id (a stable descending argsort -- the same canonical order as topk_softmax; load-bearing for bf16-induced ties). The masked-out tokens get probability 0; the kept top-k are renormalized to sum to 1; then inverse-CDF sampling (the draw can never land on a masked token)
current Triton lowering loads one full row per program (BLOCK_V=next_pow2(V)); the production 100k+ vocab path needs a staged streaming scan (tuning follow-up, NOT a contract change)
top_k is a SCALAR (one value for the whole batch); per-row tensor top_k is a follow-up (it breaks the TOPK constexpr unroll in the device kernel)
top-p / nucleus + combined top-k-top-p sampling are SEPARATE ops (they need a device sort) and are a follow-up

numerics.notes: Token selection is INTEGER-EXACT (rtol=atol=0). The top-k selection is the same descending-prob/ascending-id-tiebreak canonical order as topk_softmax (integer-exact); the mask+renorm is deterministic fp32; the inverse-CDF draw runs on a fixed-point int64 representation (see sampling_from_probs@1.0.0 numerics.notes) so it is bit-exact across scan orders/backends. The whole op is deterministic given (probs, uniform_samples, top_k).
